manifest.title: Configurator Example
manifest.description: Exercises the `configurator` item type (E5). A configurator targets the `summary` table by its stable instance id; the resolver validates the reference and auto-generates an editor form from the table's configurable surface — one control per surface field (`title`, `columns`, `query`), each picked by the field's preferred `rendering` or the canonical widget for its value type. The generated controls are attached to the resolved configurator node and bind the `summary.<field>` config-override address, so changing one posts an ephemeral config override that re-resolves the target. The author writes no per-field form: the editor is derived purely from what the table declares as runtime-configurable. Conforms to https://lattice.dev/schemas/dashboard/1.0.0.
manifest.author: Lattice
